titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the E.L. McGlashan Collection of Documents Concerning Slavery in the United States
McGlashan (E.L) Collection of Documents Concerning Slavery in the United States
titlestmt.author: by Kathleen T. Burns

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[E. L. MCGLASHAN COLLECTION OF DOCUMENTS CONCERNING SLAVERY IN THE UNITED STATES ])//EN" "mcglasha.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

did.unittitle: E. L. McGlashan collection of documents concerning slavery in the United States

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection of 46 documents consists of manuscript bills of sale, receipts, estate appraisals, deeds of gift, manumission statements, promissory notes, and other papers documenting slave ownership and the slave trade in the United States between 1770 and 1863. The bulk of the material documents slavery in North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, Georgia, Kentucky and Alabama. There are also legal documents recording slavery transactions in Maine, Tennessee, Pennsylvania, and Louisiana.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Scott McGlashan to the Gilder Lehrman Center of Yale University. Gift of the Gilder Lehrman Center of Yale University, 2005.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The E. L. McGlashan Collection of Documents Concerning Slavery in the United States is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: The E. L. McGlashan Collection of Documents Concerning Slavery in the United States. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: E. L. MCGLASHAN (1911-1978)
bioghist.p: Everitt Lynn McGlashan was born in Perth, New York in 1911. A collector of Americana, his focus was on antiquarian books, archives, and ephemera documenting the American South and West. Most of the documents in McGlashan's collection of slavery material were purchased from secondhand bookstores in the nineteen sixties during family vacations in Richmond, Atlanta, New Orleans and other southern cities. McGlashan died in North Tarrytown, New York in 1978.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The E. L. McGlashan Collection of Papers Concerning Slavery in the United States consists of bills of sale, receipts, estate records, and other material documenting slave ownership and the slave trade in the United States. The papers span the dates 1770-1862, and predominantly document transactions in North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, Georgia, Kentucky, and Alabama. There are also records which document legal actions involving slaves in Maine, Tennessee, Pennsylvania, and Louisiana.

Series I. Bills of Sale
is arranged chronologically and consists of 11 documents. The folder titles consist of transcribed information, and include the names of sellers, buyers, and slaves, as well as the dates of sales. The jurisdiction of sales has been transcribed when recorded. These documents also typically include the amount for which the slaves were sold; a witness statement; and the date of the sale's recording at the country clerk's office or other official registry. Some sales include description of the skills, age, physical condition and family relationships of the slaves. In many cases, annotations on the verso record subsequent transactions. Some records also provide information about the seller. The 1840 bill of sale for slaves sold by Antoine Lacour of Iberville Parish, Louisiana (folder 11) notes that the Lacour is a "free man of color."

Series II. Receipts
is arranged chronologically and consists of 11 documents. The receipts usually include information about the sale, including the amount of money paid, the name of the slave, and the date of the sale. Some receipts also include a description of the physical condition and family relationships of the slaves being sold. Several of the receipts in this collection are recorded on preprinted forms completed in manuscript (folders 13, 14, 17, 18).

Series III. Estate Records
is arranged chronologically and consists of property appraisals and inventories of the estates of deceased individuals in various Alabama counties between 1843 and 1860. Inventories typically list the names of slaves and their valuation, as well as descriptions and valuation amounts for other personal property owned by the deceased. The records are annotated with witness statements, attestations, and often bear notes regarding the administration of the estate and the final division of property.

Series IV. Other Documents
is arranged chronologically and consists of 16 manuscript and printed items related to slavery in the United States. The series includes deeds gifting slaves to relatives (folders 32, 36, 39, 41, 44), contracts for hiring out slave labor (folders 33, 35), and manumission statements, including one document that records the registering of three mulatto boys and their slave mother shortly after Pennsylvania's 1780 Gradual Abolition of Slavery Act (folder 31).

Documents in this collection were already numbered using a three character alphanumeric code when the materials were acquired by the Beinecke Library in 2005. The Gilder Lehrman Center used these codes in citations for items from this collection that were digitized for the Center's workshop "Sound as a Dollar: The Paperwork of Chattel Slavery." The codes have been retained in this finding aid and are listed in parentheses following the folder description.

did.unittitle: Bills of Sale
Gray, Stevens of Bertie County, North Carolina to Edward Turner, "one Negro woman named Priss" (C25)
Rhodes, John of Bertie County, North Carolina to Joshua Davis, [Glasgow?] County, "Negro man named Jacob" (C30)
Ferrell, Jephthah of Wake County, North Carolina to Patrick Terrell, "Negro boy named Jack about seventeen years old" (C1)
Warrin, Pleasant F. of Halifax County, Virginia to Mary Fulcher of Person County, North Carolina, "Negro boy about four years old named Gilbred" (C29)
Boughton, Thomas of Essex County, [Virginia?] to Joseph Janey, "one Negroe woman named Halse" (H3)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verso notations record sale of Halse by Janey six days later to Abraham Shepherd and Shepherd's sale to John McDonogh and Shepherd Brown in New Orleans on 1708 Feb 10

did.unittitle: Jones, William and Nathaniel Howcott, Chowan County, North Carolina, "four Negroes, names as follows Rachel, Biner, Steaven, and Hannah" (H25)
Satterfield, David of Person County, North Carolina to Jesse Lunsford, "a certain Negro boy by the name of Sam of a yellow complexion about ten years old" (C28)
Haley, James H. of Wake County, North Carolina to Burrell Perry, "a certain Negro named Anthony about fifteen years of age" (C7)
Hill, Benjamin of [P. County?] to Williamson Harpe, "one Negro girl named Becky, five years old" (C9)
Hanlin, William of Hickman County, Tennessee to Lawson H. Nunnely, "a certain Negro boy named Andy about thirteen years of age" (H84)

Lacour, Antoine of Iberville Parish, Louisiana to Macques Molaison Jr. of West Baton Rouge Parish, Louisiana, "two slaves... Peter, a Negro man aged thirty years, and Paul, a Negro man also aged thirty years" (C39)
Receipts
Davis, John. Receipt for "two Negroes, Jacob and Sam" from Thomas H. Anderson (H77)
Perry, Willie. Receipt for "purchase of one Negro slave named Zena," sold to E. H. Simmons. Richmond, [Virginia] (C4)
Miller, D. M. Receipt for "purchase of Negro slave named Moses," sold to E. H. Simmons. Richmond, [Virginia] (C23)

[Bloodgood?], John. Receipt for "Madison and his wife Nancy, and two children, also Patty and an old woman... also Stephen [Hitchow?], his wife Hester," sold to Joseph Lundry. Montgomery, [Alabama] (H75)
Sumner, Benjamin. Receipt for purchase of "one Negroe slave named Priscilla aged about twenty-two years," sold to E. H. Simmons. Lincoln County, North Carolina (C8)
Campbell, B. M. & W. L. Receipt for sale of "four Negro slaves named Betsy and her three children... Rachel, Clea, and Eliza" to E. H. Simmons. Baltimore, [Maryland] (C2)
Hall, William. W. Receipt for "purchase of a Negro slave named Mary and her child Louisianna," sold to Charles H. Shield. Norfolk, [Virginia] (H61)
Harrison, C. A. Receipt for sale of "boy, Dick, aged about seven years," sold to W. W. Holden. Wake County, North Carolina (C6)
Spins, W. Receipt for sale of "a Negro man named Peter," sold to E. H. Simmons. Hamburg (C3)
Marlen, W. L. Receipts for two slaves sold to John H. Newton. Jackson County, Georgia (H26, H27)
Estate Records
Bethen, Hugh. Monroe County, Alabama (H79)
Young, Hugh. Wilcox County, Alabama (H81)
Sellers, Samuel. Wilcox County, Alabama (H82)
Evans Law, John Alex. Wilcox County, Alabama (H76)
Burton, Ephraim T. Greene County, Alabama (C32, C33)
Haddox, Catherine. Wilcox County, Alabama (H80)
Other Documents
Martin, Jacob. Manumission document for a "Negro manservant named Boston," [Biddeford, Maine?] (M12)
Boyd, William of Lancaster, Pennsylvania. Record of registration for three mulatto children and their mother (C22)
Dargan, Ann. Deed of gift and sale for "the moiety of the Negro man slave named Virginia" to nephew William Moon, Bertie County, North Carolina (C26)
Nicohols, Zachariah. Contract for "the hire of a negro Pearse for the ensuing year" (C21)
Henry Gorin acting for William W. Whitaker. Execution against property form, issued against [Lewith Lofland, Iavano Cromwell, Charles Lofland, and Lamuel Hill?]. Logan County, Kentucky (H16)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verso bears notations for carrying out payment of debt, including sale of slaves

did.unittitle: Brooks, R. S. and R. J. [Vallant?]. Contract for "the hire [of] Negro boy Wright," from William Bibb, [Huntsville, Alabama] (C35)
Harward, John. Deed of gift for "one Negro girl by the name of Marget" to his son, John Harward, Wake County, North Carolina (C5)
Bonan, John and James Petty. Promissory note for purchase of "Negro man name[d] Ben" (C10)
Clagett, Richard. "Public Sale of Negroes," Potters Mart, Charleston, South Carolina, printed broadside (C38)

[Gains, Janus?] of Randolph County, North Carolina. Deed of gift for "one Negro man named Isham about twenty years old, one Negro woman named Carolina about twenty-five years old, and another Negro woman named Uma and her child Alfred," to his daughter, Julia E. [Armstead?] of Wilcox County, Alabama (H78)
"Inventory and sale of 1/6 Negro man, Moses, and 1/6 part of Negro woman, [Pheriley?]... Richard C. Fulford's estate," manuscript copy (C37)
Walker, Moses of Person County, North Carolina. Deed of gift for "one Negro man Ferryman, one boy Frank," to his son, Alexander Walker of Halifax County, Virginia (C27)
Burwell, Cornelius and T. J. Execution against property form, issued against estate of H. W. Wells and Thomas R. Wells. Logan County, Kentucky (H17)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attached document dated 1859 May 13 records transfer of "a Negro boy" to sheriff in payment of debt

did.unittitle: [Ramsey?], Joseph B. Note relating to capture of escaped slave, Augusta, [Georgia] (C36)
Brown, Burwell of Wake County, North Carolina. Deed of gift for "the following slaves Caroline, Henry, Mary, Alek, William," to his son-in-law, Richard J. Holt (C11)
Harris, James M. for S. Tinsley. Tax certificate, printed form completed in manuscript, Bedford County, [Virginia] (C12)
Wells, Irby S. Duplicate receipt related to claim for compensation against the Confederate government for loss of a slave employed in military fortifications, Columbia, South Carolina (C16)
